## Historical Context Summary: Dylan Carlson (June 15, 2018)

**At Time of Purchase (June 2018):** Carlson had just been promoted from Peoria after batting .234 with two home runs in 13 games and was playing for Palm Beach in the Florida State League, where he would finish the year slashing .247/.345/.386 with nine home runs and 53 RBI in 99 games. His .738 combined OPS was the highest mark of his career at that point, signaling early improvement. At just 18 years old, he was playing at a level teams are extremely hesitant to send college juniors to directly after the draft—a strong developmental signal for a 1st-round pick from 2016.

**Why It Succeeded:** In 2019, just over a year after the purchase date, Carlson finished with career bests in nearly every offensive category (.292 average, 26 homers, 68 RBI, 20 stolen bases) and was named Texas League Player of the Year and Cardinals Minor League Player of the Year. He made his MLB debut on August 15, 2020, and in 2021, his rookie season, he slashed .266/.343/.437 with 18 home runs,
